methods.description: This method involves comparing paleoenvironmental estimates with simulated conditions to verify climate models. It focuses on using sub-orbital resolution verification data around a specific stratigraphic target to reduce uncertainty.

steps.description: Adopt an initial time slice centered at 3.205 Ma for generating short time series for comparison with PlioMIP2 experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: select time slice at 3.205 Ma for data generation

steps.description: Generate short high-resolution time series between MIS M2 and MIS KM3.
steps.searchable_summary: generate high-resolution time series between MIS M2 and MIS KM3

steps.description: Use independent means of correlation through the oxygen isotope record to achieve suborbital chronologic resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate using oxygen isotope record for suborbital resolution

steps.description: Compare transient simulations to time series to provide information on Piacenzian climate change variability and dynamics.
steps.searchable_summary: compare transient simulations with time series for climate change analysis
outputs: climate change variability and dynamics information

methods.description: This method focuses on integrating multivariate paleontological and geochemical data for a holistic paleoenvironmental reconstruction, moving away from single-variable analysis.

steps.description: Identify localities suitable for high-resolution marine-terrestrial correlation.
Utilize multivariate data sets for a holistic paleoenvironmental reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze multivariate data for holistic reconstruction

steps.description: Compare Earth system model outputs with Piacenzian reconstructions using multiple lines of independent geological evidence.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model outputs with Piacenzian reconstructions
